Same outward behavior as s07: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in, agent reply out.
The diff is internal: the system prompt is now non-empty.

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo -skills weather,echo -v "test"
[s08] bootstrap files present: 0/4 ([AGENTS.md SOUL.md USER.md TOOLS.md])
[s08] loaded 2 skill(s) from workspace="/tmp/agent-demo/skills" builtin=".../testdata/skills"
       - echo
       - weather
[s08] skills requested for system prompt: [weather echo]
[s08] system prompt: 612 chars; messages: 1
...

Adding a SOUL.md flips the bootstrap count and grows the system prompt:

$ cat > /tmp/agent-demo/SOUL.md <<'EOF'
You are a terse weather-savvy assistant. Always respond in 2 lines or less.
EOF

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo -skills weather -v "Tokyo"
[s08] bootstrap files present: 1/4 ([AGENTS.md SOUL.md USER.md TOOLS.md])
[s08] loaded 2 skill(s) ...
[s08] skills requested for system prompt: [weather]
[s08] system prompt: 893 chars; messages: 1
...

The system prompt grows from 612 → 893 chars because the SOUL.md content
plus its `## SOUL.md` header now sit in front of the Skills + Runtime
sections.

The runtime block always closes the system prompt:

   ...
   ---
   [Runtime Context — metadata only]
   Current Time: 2026-05-09T14:35:01Z
   Channel: cli
   Sender: yeding
   [/Runtime Context]

If you stuff the user input with `<think>` blocks, they're stripped
at the single sanitize boundary in BuildMessages — the Anthropic API
will only see the visible text:

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo "<think>secret</think>visible"
                                       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
                                       stripped before reaching the LLM

Concurrent behavior:

$ go test -count=1 -race ./...
ok      learn-nanobot/s08       0.5s

The 7 tests pin down: bootstrap-file inclusion, runtime block presence,
message-count trim, sanitize correctness on user + history text, no-skills
path, token-budget trim, and sanitize idempotence.
